items.name: Positional Possession
items.tag_line: Keep the ball to control the game, moving the opponent's shape until it breaks.
items.core_idea: Keep the ball to control the game, move the opponent's shape until it breaks, and counterpress instantly on loss so the opponent never transitions. Build-up is short from the goalkeeper. The transition rule is a five-second swarm the moment the ball is lost. Player demands: passing range and positional discipline throughout, built around a true single pivot. Risk profile: vulnerable to elite counters if rest defence is sloppy.
items.youth_takeaway: Teach the team to occupy zones and form triangles, and the free man appears from structure, not from individual talent.

tolerated: Chips and reverse passes in the final third
pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: Slow-slow-fast: circulate patiently, and accelerate only through the gaps that open.

items.name: Counter-Attack Pace
items.tag_line: Concede the ball on purpose, defend compactly, and win it back to attack in under ten seconds.
items.core_idea: Concede the ball on purpose, defend a compact mid-to-low block, and win it to go for the throat in under ten seconds. Build-up is direct and vertical with minimal passes. The transition rule: win it, then the first pass goes forward with at least two runners. Player demands: elite pace in the front three, a destroyer at six, and a transition ten. Risk profile: struggles to create when forced to hold the ball, since an organised opponent can simply park instead.
items.youth_takeaway: A team does not need the ball to be dangerous: know exactly who sprints and where the instant it is won back.

tolerated: F13 channel balls to relieve pressure
discouraged: Slow lateral circulation in transition, wasting the regain
pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: Fast on the regain, inside ten seconds; calm reset if the window closes.

items.name: Gegenpress / High-Intensity
items.tag_line: Suffocate opponents high up the pitch and score before they can reset.
items.core_idea: The press is the playmaker: suffocate opponents high, win the ball thirty metres from goal, and score before they reset. Build-up is fast and forward, comfortable going direct to skip the press phase. The transition rule works both ways: counterpress on loss, instant vertical play on regain. Player demands: a high pressing engine across the front six, high work rate both ways, a high-line centre back pairing with recovery pace, and a sweeper keeper. Risk profile: physically brutal, and one broken press line means a one-against-one with the goalkeeper.
items.youth_takeaway: Effort can be a system, not just a vibe: pressing has choreography, with curved runs and triggers, that can be trained like a passing drill.

encouraged: quick F7 through balls after regains
tolerated: Direct F8 to skip a press phase
discouraged: Square or back passes under no pressure
pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: Always forward-first: possession is a means, not a target to protect.

items.name: Low-Block Counter
items.tag_line: Two disciplined banks with zero space between the lines, then release a sprinter into open grass.
items.core_idea: Two banks, zero space between the lines, total positional discipline, then release one or two sprinters into sixty metres of grass. Player demands: discipline everywhere, an outlet striker, and at least one game-breaking runner. Risk profile: thin margins, where one set-piece concession can end the game plan.
items.youth_takeaway: Defending deep is a plan, not a surrender: never send a block out without an outlet to break with.

encouraged: F8 over the top when sprung
tolerated: Anything that gains ten seconds of rest for the block
discouraged: Risky short build-up near your own box
pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: Two speeds only: dead slow with the ball to kill tempo, and maximal on the break.

items.name: Direct & Second Ball
items.tag_line: Go long to a target early and structure the team around winning the second ball.
items.core_idea: Play the percentages: go long to a target early, structure the team to win the second ball, and camp in the opponent's half. The approach is aggressive, physical, and front-foot, with a mid block that counterpresses second balls hard. Player demands: a target man, aerial eights, and pressing engine everywhere. Risk profile: low control, and vulnerable to a calm build-up team if the first press wave misses.
items.youth_takeaway: Winning the loose ball after a long pass is a team job that can be organised and drilled, not left to chance.

pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: High constant tempo: the ball goes forward, or it goes long.

items.name: Hybrid / Transition Control
items.tag_line: Comfortable in possession and in a defensive block, switching approach by opponent and game state.
items.core_idea: The modern winner's profile: comfortable in possession and in a block, choosing an approach per opponent and per game state. Baseline is a solid mid-block plus elite transitions, with the ball held to kill games when needed. Player demands: tactical intelligence over any single physical trait, and versatile roles, such as a ten who defends or a defensive fullback opposite an attacking one. Realistically the sanest default identity for a youth team.
items.youth_takeaway: Teams do not have to pick a single lane: the sanest identity for most youth sides is one that can do both.

encouraged: The possession menu when controlling the game
discouraged: Nothing categorically: decision quality is the graded skill
pass_risk_json.tempo_rule: Game-state driven: winning uses the possession menu, chasing uses the counter menu.
